name: Bidirectional Dijkstra
notes: Runs two uniform-cost searches until their settled frontiers certify a shortest path.

name: K-Shortest Paths (Yen)
notes: Enumerates loopless alternatives after the first shortest route.

name: Eppstein K-Shortest Paths
time: O(E + V log V + K log K)
notes: Uses sidetrack edges to enumerate many near-shortest routes efficiently.

name: Adaptive A*
notes: Reuses previous search effort by updating heuristic values after each run.

name: Real-Time A*
notes: Interleaves acting and bounded local search for online grid navigation.

name: Learning Real-Time A*
notes: Improves its heuristic while moving through repeated maze trials.

name: RTAA*
notes: Updates heuristic values after a bounded A* lookahead.

name: Anytime Dynamic A*
notes: Combines anytime weighted search with incremental graph repair.

name: A*epsilon
notes: Uses focal search to trade exact optimality for faster bounded solutions.

name: Multi-Heuristic A*
notes: Runs multiple heuristic queues anchored by an admissible baseline.

name: Safe Interval Path Planning
notes: Plans through cells only during collision-free time intervals.

name: Cooperative A*
notes: Searches a reservation-table grid to avoid other moving agents.

name: Windowed Hierarchical Cooperative A*
notes: Limits cooperative search to a rolling time window.

name: Conflict-Based Search
notes: Splits agent conflicts into constraints and replans individual paths.

name: Visibility Graph Shortest Path
notes: Connects visible obstacle vertices before running shortest-path search.

name: Exact Cellular Decomposition
notes: Partitions free space into exact cells and searches their adjacency graph.

name: Boustrophedon Decomposition
notes: Sweeps a line through planar free space to build coverage/navigation cells.

name: Trapezoidal Decomposition
notes: Builds a planar roadmap from vertical decomposition of polygonal obstacles.

name: Bug 0
notes: Moves toward the goal, then follows obstacle boundaries when blocked.

name: DistBug
notes: Leaves obstacle boundaries when distance and visibility criteria improve.

name: VisBug
notes: Uses visibility information while navigating obstacle boundaries.

name: RangeBug
notes: Uses range sensing to choose leave points during boundary following.

name: Vector Field Histogram
notes: Builds polar obstacle histograms for local steering decisions.

name: Elastic Bands
notes: Deforms a route under obstacle and smoothing forces.

name: Timed Elastic Band
notes: Optimizes both path geometry and timing constraints.
reference: Rosmann et al. TEB local planner

name: Dynamic Roadmap
notes: Updates roadmap validity as obstacles or costs change.

name: Lazy PRM
notes: Delays edge collision checks until a candidate route needs them.

name: PRM*
notes: Asymptotically optimal probabilistic roadmap construction.

name: RRT-Connect
notes: Grows two rapidly-exploring trees toward each other.

name: RRG
notes: Rapidly-exploring random graph with asymptotic optimality guarantees.

name: RRT#
notes: Maintains shortest-path consistency values over an RRT-like graph.

name: RRTX
notes: Repairs an asymptotically optimal tree in dynamic environments.

name: EST
notes: Expansive-space tree sampling favors unexplored regions.

name: SBL
notes: Single-query bidirectional lazy collision-checking planner.

name: FMT*
notes: Fast marching tree method over random geometric graphs.

name: ABIT*
notes: Advanced batched informed trees improve anytime sampling behavior.

name: Ordered Upwind Method
notes: Solves anisotropic front propagation for continuous cost fields.

name: Fast Iterative Method
notes: Iteratively solves Eikonal equations for distance fields.

name: Particle Swarm Optimization
notes: Optimizes waypoint paths with swarm velocity updates.

name: Cross-Entropy Method
notes: Samples candidate paths and updates a distribution toward elite routes.

name: Network Simplex Path Formulation
notes: Solves shortest path as a network-flow linear optimization problem.

name: CP-SAT Path Encoding
notes: Encodes path feasibility with integer and Boolean constraints.

name: SMT Path Encoding
notes: Uses satisfiability modulo theories for bounded grid-path constraints.
